# PSFTP Response Golden Vectors

These vectors characterize higher-level PSFTP request and write response behavior before KMP migration. They pin response reassembly, response-error mapping, write progress policy, peer interruption, first-packet transport failure, and write-ack timeout planning. `PsFtpRuntimePolicyCommonTest.kt` now executes the deterministic shared response/write policy subset with typed response, write progress platform-split pinning, transport, and write-ack-timeout failures; production shared runtime still needs fake MTU/write-ack transport integration, facade-compatible progress behavior, cancellation cleanup, fake-clock coverage, and facade error mapping before PSFTP write orchestration moves to common code.

`psftp-runtime-readiness.json` names the pre-migration behavior-family gate for PSFTP runtime extraction across response, notification, and write vectors: `request-response-reassembly`, notification reassembly, response-error mapping, notification ordering, initial-silence no-timeout behavior, consumer-timeout observer cleanup, notification error and transport-status platform split, continuation timeout, write progress platform split, write interruption, write transport failure, write-ack timeout, fake-clock timeout gates, platform client vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production PSFTP runtime orchestration moves into shared KMP.
